comments.creator: ryanvm@gmail.com
comments.author: ryanvm@gmail.com

comments.raw_text: Yep, as the name of the signature suggests, this is expected behaviour, in theory. It is possible we crash in circumstances that we didn't expect, but this is what will now happen if we fail to restart the GPU process enough times when we previously had a stable GPU process. We should brace ourselves for increasing numbers as this hits further channels.
comments.text: Yep, as the name of the signature suggests, this is expected behaviour, in theory. It is possible we crash in circumstances that we didn't expect, but this is what will now happen if we fail to restart the GPU process enough times when we previously had a stable GPU process. We should brace ourselves for increasing numbers as this hits further channels.

comments.raw_text: Most of these crashes appear to be happening because the launching is failing. Given the numbers are higher on Android, this does make a case for getting bug 1991502 working there too.
comments.text: Most of these crashes appear to be happening because the launching is failing. Given the numbers are higher on Android, this does make a case for getting bug 1991502 working there too.

comments.author: jnicol@mozilla.com
comments.creator: jnicol@mozilla.com
comments.text: Today I was additionally looking at getting some more information about why the process launch failed annotated in the crash reports. Hopefully have something up tomorrow. Likely won't tell us anything particularly useful, but worth a shot
comments.raw_text: Today I was additionally looking at getting some more information about why the process launch failed annotated in the crash reports. Hopefully have something up tomorrow. Likely won't tell us anything particularly useful, but worth a shot

Some of the android crashes now have the annotation from bug 1992951. (Most don't have: perhaps the error in these cases does not come from the java code, or there is an issue with the annotation.) But the ones which do have this message:

> Error launching GPU process: Error while binding: android.os.DeadObjectException;

I expect this therefore originates from the `Context.bindService()` call which is called from within [this call](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/source/mobile/android/geckoview/src/main/java/org/mozilla/gecko/process/GeckoProcessManager.java#923), and indicates that the process is already dead by this point.

Perhaps the new process was immediately killed (eg due to low resources)? Or, perhaps the `getConnectionForStart()` call [immediately above](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/source/mobile/android/geckoview/src/main/java/org/mozilla/gecko/process/GeckoProcessManager.java#921) is returning the stale `Connection` for the process that _previously_ crashed (ie the one for which we are attempting to launch its replacement).

It grabs an [existing connection](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/source/mobile/android/geckoview/src/main/java/org/mozilla/gecko/process/GeckoProcessManager.java#658) from `mNonContentConnections`, if one exists. The connection for the previous process should get removed in [`removeConnection`](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/91e6069b9c1e7289d0c727b5110e144edc589204/mobile/android/geckoview/src/main/java/org/mozilla/gecko/process/GeckoProcessManager.java#582), which is called from [`unbindService`](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/91e6069b9c1e7289d0c727b5110e144edc589204/mobile/android/geckoview/src/main/java/org/mozilla/gecko/process/ServiceAllocator.java#266), which gets called from the [`onBinderConnectionLost` callback](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/91e6069b9c1e7289d0c727b5110e144edc589204/mobile/android/geckoview/src/main/java/org/mozilla/gecko/process/ServiceAllocator.java#303). Perhaps that callback being fired is racing with gecko detecting the process loss and relaunching it.
comments.text:
Some of the android crashes now have the annotation from bug 1992951. (Most don't have: perhaps the error in these cases does not come from the java code, or there is an issue with the annotation.) But the ones which do have this message:

> Error launching GPU process: Error while binding: android.os.DeadObjectException;

I expect this therefore originates from the `Context.bindService()` call which is called from within [this call](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/source/mobile/android/geckoview/src/main/java/org/mozilla/gecko/process/GeckoProcessManager.java#923), and indicates that the process is already dead by this point.

Perhaps the new process was immediately killed (eg due to low resources)? Or, perhaps the `getConnectionForStart()` call [immediately above](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/source/mobile/android/geckoview/src/main/java/org/mozilla/gecko/process/GeckoProcessManager.java#921) is returning the stale `Connection` for the process that _previously_ crashed (ie the one for which we are attempting to launch its replacement).

It grabs an [existing connection](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/source/mobile/android/geckoview/src/main/java/org/mozilla/gecko/process/GeckoProcessManager.java#658) from `mNonContentConnections`, if one exists. The connection for the previous process should get removed in [`removeConnection`](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/91e6069b9c1e7289d0c727b5110e144edc589204/mobile/android/geckoview/src/main/java/org/mozilla/gecko/process/GeckoProcessManager.java#582), which is called from [`unbindService`](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/91e6069b9c1e7289d0c727b5110e144edc589204/mobile/android/geckoview/src/main/java/org/mozilla/gecko/process/ServiceAllocator.java#266), which gets called from the [`onBinderConnectionLost` callback](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/91e6069b9c1e7289d0c727b5110e144edc589204/mobile/android/geckoview/src/main/java/org/mozilla/gecko/process/ServiceAllocator.java#303). Perhaps that callback being fired is racing with gecko detecting the process loss and relaunching it.
comments.author: jnicol@mozilla.com

comments.raw_text:
The bug is linked to a topcrash signature, which matches the following [criterion](https://wiki.mozilla.org/CrashKill/Topcrash):
- Top 10 AArch64 and ARM crashes on nightly 

:aosmond, could you consider increasing the severity of this top-crash bug?

For more information, please visit [BugBot documentation](https://wiki.mozilla.org/BugBot#topcrash_highlight.py).

comments.creator: release-mgmt-account-bot@mozilla.tld
comments.author: release-mgmt-account-bot@mozilla.tld

comments.text: As we make improvements to the GPU process management, we hope to either reduce the number of these crashes, or embrace them if Firefox was going to crash soon anyways. For the moment these crashes are limited to nightly due to the pref, so I don't think the topcrash concern is warranted.
comments.raw_text: As we make improvements to the GPU process management, we hope to either reduce the number of these crashes, or embrace them if Firefox was going to crash soon anyways. For the moment these crashes are limited to nightly due to the pref, so I don't think the topcrash concern is warranted.
comments.author: aosmond@mozilla.com
comments.creator: aosmond@mozilla.com

comments.text:
Created attachment 9539111
Bug 1992573 - Disable crashing if compositing falls back to the parent process in nightly.


We only crash in nightly right now, and since we aren't actively working
on getting rid of parent process compositing at this moment, let's just
disable this for convenience.
